title: Why QR Codes Are a Trust Problem, Not a Tech Problem
summary: The risk in QR codes is not the square itself; it is the invisible destination and rushed context.

title: One Red Flag Can Save You
summary: You do not need perfect detection; one credible warning sign is enough reason to stop and verify.

title: Why Smart People Still Get Scammed
summary: Scams succeed through context and pressure, not lack of intelligence.

title: Move Fast, Ship Risk: A Builder’s Tradeoff
summary: Shipping quickly is not the problem; shipping without secure defaults is.

title: AI Makes Scams Cheaper, Faster, and More Personal
summary: Generative tooling lowers attacker cost and increases iteration speed, changing scam economics.

title: Good Security Advice Should Be Clear Under Stress
summary: If guidance only works when people are calm and technical, it is not good enough.
